title: Empirical Fresh-Tyre Premium Features Instead Of Static Compound Lifespan Rules
summary: Public F1 strategy writeups stress that undercut vs overcut depends on track-specific warm-up and degradation, not just nominal compound life.
rationale: Public F1 strategy writeups stress that undercut vs overcut depends on track-specific warm-up and degradation, not just nominal compound life. Your current best features use fixed expected-life windows; a stronger test is to estimate the race-specific value of fresh tyres from observed laps in that same event.
implementation_hint: For each `Race_Year x Compound x LapNumber` or broader race-phase bucket, estimate the recent pace residual of cars just after a pit stop versus cars staying out, using only past laps within fold-safe ordering. Build features like `FreshTyrePremium`, `OutLapPenalty`, `StaleTyrePenalty`, and `UndercutStrengthIndex`; interact them with the driver’s current stint age and position.
expected_effect: Should capture why the same tyre age triggers a stop in one race but not another, especially across low-deg overcut tracks versus high-deg undercut tracks.
risk: Requires strict causal construction; if computed with future laps or full-race statistics it will leak badly.
